Cherry Hill Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram

top10.introHtml: Best picks for a cheap, fun second car — prioritizing reliable convertible platforms, low mileage for the price, and proximity to Media, PA. The Mazda Miata dominates for a reason: it's cheap to own, nearly indestructible, and a blast to drive. BMWs and MINIs round out the list with more style. PPI everything — convertible top condition is the make-or-break factor at this price point.

picks.dealer: Corsinos Service Station and Repair Inc
picks.whyGood: 1987 Ford Mustang at 98,916 miles for $9,500. Rated Great Deal. Clean title and no accidents per listing.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle. Check headliner and carpet for water stains. Inspect rear-window seam and rocker-panel rust. Inspect convertible top mount-to-rocker frame for rust (SN95). T45/T3650 third-gear synchro slow-shift test.
picks.bottomLine: The 1987 Ford Mustang at $9,500 is worth a look — do a proper PPI before committing.

picks.whyGood: Mazda MX-5 Miata is the gold standard for cheap fun convertibles. This Grand Touring has AC, power windows, and Bilstein shocks. 177k miles is high but Miatas routinely go 200k+ if properly maintained.
picks.whatToVerify: Run timing belt history (replace if unknown — interval is 60k mi on pre-2006; 2012 is chain-driven so no belt needed). Operate the top through full open/close cycle, check for top-latch rattle, inspect trunk seals for water intrusion. Ask about clutch feel.
picks.bottomLine: Best value fun-per-dollar in the list. At $10,950 you're buying a nearly bulletproof roadster platform. Verify service history and enjoy.

picks.whyGood: 2015 MINI Cooper at 55,000 miles for $9,999.  Clean title and no accidents per listing.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle. Check headliner and carpet for water stains. Inspect rear-window seam and rocker-panel rust.
picks.bottomLine: The 2015 MINI Cooper at $9,999 is worth a look — do a proper PPI before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2006 Pontiac Solstice with only 36k miles — extremely low mileage for a 20-year-old car. This is the base 177hp inline-4. Fun chassis, good looks, and at $11,200 with clean title from Private Seller Exchange, it's competitive.
picks.whatToVerify: Check that the soft top mechanism operates smoothly — Solstice tops can be fiddly. Inspect for frame rust at rocker panels. Verify oil level (early Ecotec engines can consume oil on highway). Ask about any deferred maintenance.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mileage Solstice at a fair price. Not as proven as a Miata but a genuinely fun, American-made roadster.

picks.whyGood: 2007 Audi A4 2.0T quattro Cabriolet at 62k miles, Great Deal rating at Selden Motors. The B7 A4 Cabriolet with quattro is a solid all-weather convertible.
picks.whatToVerify: B7 A4 is prone to timing chain tensioner failure on the 2.0T — verify if addressed. Check PCV valve and oil consumption. Inspect the soft top for rear window delamination.
picks.bottomLine: Great Deal badge and relatively low miles make this competitive. Factor in German car maintenance costs when budgeting.

picks.whyGood: 2011 Audi A5 2.0T Premium Plus at just 56k miles — lowest mileage Audi in the list. The B8 A5 Cabriolet is genuinely stylish and the 2.0 TFSI is a solid engine.
picks.whatToVerify: Audi 2.0T of this era has known timing chain tensioner issues and PCV valve failures — ask for service history. Check DSG fluid if so equipped. Inspect soft top for tears and motor operation.
picks.bottomLine: Best-looking car in the list at this mileage. Ownership costs will be higher than a Miata or MINI but this is a genuinely premium experience.

picks.whyGood: 2000 BMW Z3 at 82,690 miles for $7,799.  Clean title and no accidents per listing.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle. Check headliner and carpet for water stains. Inspect rear-window seam and rocker-panel rust. Check oil for milkiness (head gasket). Verify cooling system (expansion tank, water pump). Pull codes.
picks.bottomLine: The 2000 BMW Z3 at $7,799 is worth a look — do a proper PPI before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2012 MINI Cooper at 112,117 miles for $8,389. Rated Good Deal. Clean title and no accidents per listing.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate the convertible top through a full open/close cycle. Check headliner and carpet for water stains. Inspect rear-window seam and rocker-panel rust.
picks.bottomLine: The 2012 MINI Cooper at $8,389 is worth a look — do a proper PPI before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2012 MINI Cooper at 98,000 miles for $9,400.  Solid affordable convertible option.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top through full cycle. Check carpet and headliner for water stains. Inspect rear window seam. Check rocker panels for rust.
picks.bottomLine: Worth a look at $9,400. Do a PPI focused on top condition and rust before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2007 MINI Cooper at 65,594 miles for $8,452.  Solid affordable convertible option.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate top through full cycle. Check carpet and headliner for water stains. Inspect rear window seam. Check rocker panels for rust.
picks.bottomLine: Worth a look at $8,452. Do a PPI focused on top condition and rust before committing.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles in this price range. V8 GT trims are rare under $15k but a few V6 examples with low-ish miles are available nearby. Fun American muscle with relatively affordable parts — just inspect the frame around the top mounts for rust, which is the Achilles heel of these soft-tops.

picks.dealer: Corsinos Service Station and Repair Inc
picks.whyGood: 1987 Ford Mustang GT at 98,916 miles for $9,500. Rated Great Deal. Convertible Mustangs in this price range are increasingly scarce — this one is worth a look.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect convertible-top-mount-to-rocker frame for rust (SN95/S197). T3650 third-gear synchro slow-shift test. 8.8 rear-axle clunk on takeoff. Check rear seat water staining from top seal failure. Verify top latch and rear window zipper/glass condition.
picks.bottomLine: Classic American drop-top at $9,500. Verify rust and top condition before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2012 Ford Mustang V6 Premium at 96,539 miles for $10,895. Rated Fair Deal. Convertible Mustangs in this price range are increasingly scarce — this one is worth a look.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect convertible-top-mount-to-rocker frame for rust (SN95/S197). T3650 third-gear synchro slow-shift test. 8.8 rear-axle clunk on takeoff. Check rear seat water staining from top seal failure. Verify top latch and rear window zipper/glass condition.
picks.bottomLine: Classic American drop-top at $10,895. Verify rust and top condition before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2010 Ford Mustang V6 Premium at 106,021 miles for $11,996. Rated Fair Deal. Convertible Mustangs in this price range are increasingly scarce — this one is worth a look.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect convertible-top-mount-to-rocker frame for rust (SN95/S197). T3650 third-gear synchro slow-shift test. 8.8 rear-axle clunk on takeoff. Check rear seat water staining from top seal failure. Verify top latch and rear window zipper/glass condition.
picks.bottomLine: Classic American drop-top at $11,996. Verify rust and top condition before committing.

picks.whyGood: 2012 Ford Mustang V6 Convertible at 90,000 miles for $11,900.  Convertible Mustangs in this price range are increasingly scarce — this one is worth a look.
picks.whatToVerify: Inspect convertible-top-mount-to-rocker frame for rust (SN95/S197). T3650 third-gear synchro slow-shift test. 8.8 rear-axle clunk on takeoff. Check rear seat water staining from top seal failure. Verify top latch and rear window zipper/glass condition.
picks.bottomLine: Classic American drop-top at $11,900. Verify rust and top condition before committing.
